task_completion.description: Log in to the chat application as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chat application interface is loaded, and the user profile, avatar, or sidebar indicates the active user is 'admin'.
The '#project-alpha' channel is visible in the left sidebar channel list, search results, or is the currently active open channel.

task_completion.description: Open the settings or context menu for the #project-alpha channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The channel information panel (usually on the right side) or a dropdown context menu for '#project-alpha' is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Select the option to archive the channel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Archive' button is being clicked, or an archive confirmation dialog box is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Confirm the channel has been successfully archived and not deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A system message stating the room was archived, an 'Archived' badge/icon next to the channel name, or the presence of an 'Unarchive' button. There must be no evidence of a deletion confirmation.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to archive the channel.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the chat application's web or desktop GUI, rather than terminal commands, API scripts, or curl requests.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete the channel.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no 'Delete Channel' confirmation dialogs visible, and the channel does not completely disappear from the system (it should remain accessible as an archived channel).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface clearly belongs to a functional chat application (Rocket.Chat) running in a browser or client, not a text editor or static mock-up.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target channel to be archived is exactly 'project-alpha'. The agent must be logged in using the administrator username 'admin'.
